title: Sambucus miquelii
intro: Sambucus miquelii, also known as Chinese elder or Korean elder, is a flowering plant species in the genus Sambucus. The health benefits of Sambucus miquelii include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are used to treat various conditions such as respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and alkaloids, including sambunigrin and sambunigrine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sambucus miquelii is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or syrup, and is used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Sambucus miquelii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae, commonly known as the elderberry family. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the Adoxaceae family. Sambucus miquelii is a part of the genus Sambucus, which comprises around 30 species of elderberry plants. The genus Sambucus is further classified within the order Dipsacales, and the family Adoxaceae. The classification of Sambucus miquelii is based on a combination of morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Sambucus miquelii, a plant native to East Asia, has been found to have various medicinal properties. Research suggests that extracts from Sambucus miquelii may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. However, more studies are needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and therapeutic applications. Further research may lead to the development of new treatments or supplements based on Sambucus miquelii.
benefits: Sambucus miquelii, also known as the Korean elder or Japanese elder, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to boost the immune system and protect against various diseases. The plant is said to have antiviral and antifungal properties, making it potentially useful in the treatment of certain infections. Additionally, Sambucus miquelii may have anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm this. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the health benefits and potential applications of this plant.
properties: Sambucus miquelii is known to have various therapeutic properties, primarily derived from its berries and other parts. The plant is traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems. The berries of Sambucus miquelii are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may contribute to their therapeutic effects. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. However, more research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic benefits of Sambucus miquelii.
constituents: Sambucus miquelii contains several active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids present in Sambucus miquelii may also have potential in modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the specific roles of these constituents in the plant's bioactivity. The exact composition and concentrations of these active constituents can vary depending on the plant's growing conditions and geographic location.
parts: Sambucus miquelii is a species of elder, and the medicinal parts of the plant typically include the berries, flowers, and leaves. The berries are rich in anthocyanins and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The flowers and leaves of Sambucus miquelii contain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. preparations: Sambucus miquelii is a plant species that is traditionally used in herbal preparations, particularly for its purported health benefits. side_effects: Sambucus miquelii is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. However, like many other plants, it may cause side effects in some individuals.
